summary: Stunning audience recording with 3‑D soundstage captures a standout 1980 show featuring a smoking Scarlet > Fire sequence and superb ensemble playing.
review: The flying‑mic AUD tape delivers exceptional separation, brightness and crystal‑clear clarity that makes you feel right in the venue.  Musically the night is often cited as one of ’80’s best – tight ensemble work, Phil’s soaring bass, Brent’s swirling B3 and a blazing Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain highlight the second set, while the first set stays solid overall.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are highly favorable (4‑5 stars); reviewers praise both the extraordinary sound quality and the strong musical performance, with only minor critiques of a few song choices.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great separation and fidelity, 3‑D imaging, bright, crisp and clear soundstage; occasional minor “bumps and bruises” noted but overall outstanding for an audience tape.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
Not Fade Away > Black Peter > Sugar Magnolia
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but average; second set erupts with high energy, featuring a powerful middle section and a classic closing trio (NFA → Black Peter → Sugar Magnolia). Overall pacing keeps the show engaging from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Lower in the mix than previous night but his playing remains stellar; vocals sit up‑front.
band_member_comments.Phil: Offers some of his best bass work early on, very prominent and expressive throughout most of the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: His vocal presence is upfront; occasional critique of his “Minglewood” choice and a seagull slide deemed over‑the‑top.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s B3 organ swirls strongly, showing growing strength in the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart are clear, balanced and punchy; drive the rhythm with thunderous energy.
